existing_groups.context_description: Term names 'the beginning' as the origin-point of inner-being realities — love, sin, testimony, truth, and faith — whose continuity from that beginning shapes the inner life
Term names cosmic or spiritual ruling powers — the inner being engaged through spiritual warfare, Christ's supremacy over them, and the believer's submission or resistance
Term names elementary or foundational teaching — the inner being engaged through the call to grow from first principles toward maturity

existing_groups.context_description: Term names the hand as the seat of power, custody, authority, and stewardship — the inner being engaged through being held under, delivered from, or entrusted with another's power

existing_groups.context_description: Term names the filling or consecration of the hand in priestly ordination — the inner being engaged through the inner dedication and covenant commitment involved in being set apart for God's service

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 13:33 After this thing Jeroboam did not turn from his evil way , but made priests for the high places again from among all the people . Any who would , he ordained to be priests of the high places .

2Ch 13:9 Have you not driven out the priests of the Lord , the sons of Aaron , and the Levites , and made priests for yourselves like the peoples of other lands ? Whoever comes for ordination with a young bull or seven rams becomes a priest of what are not gods .

2Ch 29:31 Then Hezekiah said , “You have now consecrated yourselves to the Lord . Come near ; bring sacrifices and thank offerings to the house of the Lord .” And the assembly brought sacrifices and thank offerings , and all who were of a willing heart brought burnt offerings .
Exo 28:41 And you shall put them on Aaron your brother , and on his sons with him, and shall anoint them and ordain them and consecrate them, that they may serve me as priests .
Exo 29:29 “The holy garments of Aaron shall be for his sons after him; they shall be anointed in them and ordained in them .
Exo 29:33 They shall eat those things with which atonement was made at their ordination and consecration , but an outsider shall not eat of them, because they are holy .
Exo 29:35 “ Thus you shall do to Aaron and to his sons , according to all that I have commanded you. Through seven days shall you ordain them,
Exo 29:9 and you shall gird Aaron and his sons with sashes and bind caps on them. And the priesthood shall be theirs by a statute forever . Thus you shall ordain Aaron and his sons .
Exo 32:29 And Moses said , “ Today you have been ordained for the service of the Lord , each one at the cost of his son and of his brother , so that he might bestow a blessing upon you this day .”

existing_groups.context_description: Term names the multiplication of sin, transgression, iniquity, evil, and abomination — the inner being morally corrupted to an escalating degree
Term names the multiplication of spiritual unfaithfulness — whoring after other gods, multiplying idolatrous altars, and escalating apostasy that provokes divine anger
Term names the multiplication of inner-being suffering — pain, sorrow, mourning, vexation, distress, fear, and grief intensified to an overwhelming degree

Term names the multiplication of wisdom, knowledge, understanding, and their counterparts — foolish excess of words, vanity of multiplied study, the breadth of mind God grants, and the limits of multiplied inquiry
Term names the multiplication of divine grace — abundant pardon, plentiful redemption, enlargement of the inner being through divine gentleness, and divinely multiplied strength for the weary

Term names multiplication as moral and covenantal consequence — increase predicated on obedience, prayer, inner posture, or wisdom reception; also divine multiplication of visions, signs, and words addressed to the inner being

existing_groups.context_description: Term names divine rule — God's sovereign dominion over nations, nature, and creation — engaging the inner being through awe, acknowledgement, trust, and the recognition of divine sovereignty

Term names human rule — the exercise, experience, or resistance of authority over others — engaging the inner being through accountability to righteousness, the moral character of rulers, and the inner response to oppression or just government
Term names the rule of the inner person over themselves — self-mastery over the spirit, passions, and the pull of sin — as the decisive form of inner-being dominion
